title_en: Introduction to Mughni al-Muhtaj and Its Author
content_en:
Mughni al-Muhtaj ila Ma'rifat Ma'ani Alfaz al-Minhaj (The Sufficient Guide to Knowing the Meanings of the Words of the Minhaj) is one of the principal large-scale reference works of Shafi'i jurisprudence. Written by Shams ad-Din Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Khatib ash-Shirbini (d. 977 AH / 1570 CE), it is a commentary on an-Nawawi's celebrated Minhaj al-Talibin, which is itself an abridgment of ar-Rafi'i's Al-Muharrar. Through this chain of commentary and abridgment, Mughni al-Muhtaj connects the student to the deepest layers of Shafi'i jurisprudential reasoning.

Ash-Shirbini was a major scholar of Ottoman Egypt who taught at the prestigious al-Azhar institution and produced several important works on Shafi'i law, the most significant being Mughni al-Muhtaj. His other major work, Al-Iqna fi Hall Alfaz Abi Shuja, serves a more introductory function, while Mughni al-Muhtaj addresses the advanced student who has already mastered the fundamentals and now seeks comprehensive treatment of Shafi'i legal questions with their evidential basis and scholarly disagreements.

An-Nawawi (Yahya ibn Sharaf an-Nawawi, 631–676 AH / 1233–1277 CE), whose Minhaj al-Talibin is the text being commented upon, was the most authoritative figure in the later Shafi'i school. Together with Rafi'i, whose views are consistently cited alongside Nawawi's throughout Mughni al-Muhtaj, an-Nawawi's positions represent the mu'tamad (relied-upon) opinions of the Shafi'i school. When an-Nawawi and ar-Rafi'i agree, that is the unambiguous position of the school. When they differ, an-Nawawi's view is given preference in most cases.

The Shafi'i school is characterized by its rigorous reliance on authenticated hadith as the primary source of legal rulings after the Quran, its careful use of analogy (qiyas) based on explicit textual rationales, and its avoidance of pure ra'y (independent opinion) without textual grounding. Imam ash-Shafi'i's Risalah established that the Sunnah of the Prophet is binding in the same way as the Quran, and that authentic hadiths must be followed even if they contradict the opinion of a senior scholar. This principle fundamentally shaped the school's approach to legal development.

Mughni al-Muhtaj covers the entire range of Shafi'i law in substantial detail, typically published in four to six large volumes. It is organized along the classical sequence: taharah, salah, zakat, sawm, hajj, financial transactions (with detailed attention to the permissibility of contracts and the avoidance of riba and gharar), family law, inheritance, criminal law, and judicial procedure. The work is both a teaching text and a scholarly reference, used by students to master the school and by scholars to verify its authoritative positions.

Ash-Shirbini's commentary style is methodical: he explains the meaning of each phrase in the Minhaj, supplies the relevant hadith or Quranic evidence, notes the opinions of earlier Shafi'i scholars (especially ar-Rafi'i and an-Nawawi), and indicates when there is scholarly disagreement within or between the schools. His treatment is less discursive than some commentaries but highly reliable as a statement of the school's established positions.

For anyone seeking mastery of Shafi'i jurisprudence, Mughni al-Muhtaj is an indispensable reference — thorough enough to resolve most practical questions, accessible enough that a well-trained student can navigate it independently, and authoritative enough that its formulations are accepted in Shafi'i legal discourse worldwide.
content_id:
Mughni al-Muhtaj ila Ma'rifat Ma'ani Alfazh al-Minhaj (Panduan yang Cukup untuk Mengetahui Makna Kata-Kata Al-Minhaj) adalah salah satu karya referensi berskala besar utama dari yurisprudensi Syafi'i, yang ditulis oleh Syams al-Din Muhammad ibn Ahmad asy-Syirbini (wafat 977 H / 1570 M), seorang ulama Mesir yang berkarya di al-Azhar. Ia adalah komentar atas Al-Minhaj karya an-Nawawi (631–676 H), yang sendiri adalah ringkasan dari Al-Muharrar karya ar-Rafi'i — sehingga Mughni al-Muhtaj merupakan lapisan ketiga dari analisis Syafi'i atas materi hukum yang telah disaring melalui dua tingkat ringkasan dan komentar sebelumnya.

Asy-Syirbini menulis Mughni al-Muhtaj sebagai komentar yang komprehensif yang menjelaskan formulasi-formulasi ringkas an-Nawawi dalam Al-Minhaj, memberikan bukti-bukti hadis untuk posisi-posisi yang an-Nawawi nyatakan tanpa bukti, merekonsiliasi pandangan-pandangan yang tampak bertentangan dari sumber-sumber Syafi'i yang berbeda, dan memberikan resolusi untuk kasus-kasus yang sulit. Luasnya membuat Mughni al-Muhtaj menjadi karya yang sangat menyeluruh bahkan untuk standar tradisi Syafi'i, yang menghasilkan komentar-komentar dengan skala yang besar.

Posisi Al-Minhaj karya an-Nawawi sebagai salah satu teks yurisprudensi Syafi'i yang paling banyak dipelajari memastikan bahwa komentar-komentar atasnya akan menjadi teks-teks referensi yang penting. Di antara komentar-komentar pada Al-Minhaj, Mughni al-Muhtaj karya asy-Syirbini dan Tuhfat al-Muhtaj karya Ibn Hajar al-Haytami berdiri sebagai yang paling otoritatif, mewakili dua pendekatan yang sedikit berbeda terhadap bahan yang sama dengan komentar-komentar yang saling melengkapi satu sama lain.
content_ar:
في كتاب "مغني المحتاج إلى معرفة معاني ألفاظ المنهاج"، يستعرض هذا الجزء أبوابًا من أبواب الفقه الشافعي بشرح وافٍ ومناقشة دقيقة مما له صلة بالموضوع المعنون أعلاه، مستعينًا بالأدلة الشرعية من الكتاب والسنة وأقوال الأئمة والعلماء.

ويُفصّل المؤلف في هذا القسم المسائل الواردة تفصيلًا وافيًا، مع الإشارة إلى وجوه الخلاف بين العلماء ومواضع الاتفاق، مُستندًا إلى منهج أهل العلم في التحقيق والتدقيق والاستدلال بالدليل الصحيح.

ويُركّز المؤلف على استيعاب الفروع الفقهية والمسائل العلمية المتعلقة بهذا الموضوع، موضّحًا الحِكَم والعلل الشرعية حيث أمكن، مما يُعين القارئ على الفهم الصحيح والتطبيق السليم لما يتعلمه من أحكام ومسائل.

ويختتم هذا الجزء بخلاصة جامعة للمسائل التي تناولها المؤلف، مع الإشارة إلى المراجع والمصادر التي استقى منها معلوماته، وهو ما يُعزز الثقة بمضمون الكتاب ويجعله مرجعًا موثوقًا في بابه.
